description: A natural chamber or passage within rock or earth is a 'nowhu' when it opens to the surface. The word reaches from a shallow hollow to a connected underground system. Built rooms and excavated tunnels take their ordinary made-place descriptions.
articulatory_notes: Begin with the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carrying nasal airflow into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. For unvoiced 'wh', round the lips while raising the back of the tongue without closing the passage; let the breath continue into high-back rounded 'u'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Caves have supplied shelter, storage, meeting places, and surfaces for art. People using them had to know where water and animals moved, how smoke travelled, and which passages stayed safe in darkness.

semantic_domains.nature: a naturally formed chamber within earth or rock
semantic_domains.spatial: an interior place reached through an opening in the ground or stone
